headline_question: Which TDC building blocks move with the baseline shock, and which omitted treatment legs actually change the residual most when removed?

takeaways: This audit separates direct baseline-shock responses of the TDC building blocks from residual shifts created by removing whole treatment legs.
Use the direct component responses to see which TDC pieces move with the baseline shock, and use the variant-removal diagnostics to see which omitted legs actually change the residual most.

Quarter-level construction alignment is exact for the public-component reconstruction and all currently audited implied legs, so the remaining issue is dynamic interpretation rather than arithmetic mismatch.
At h0, the largest signed direct component contribution comes from `treasury_operating_cash_drain`.
At h0, removing Treasury operating cash from the treatment shifts the residual by about 50.33.
At h0, removing Treasury operating cash and ROW together shifts the residual by about 67.24.
At h0, removing only ROW shifts the residual by about 19.97, while removing only foreign bank-sector Treasury legs shifts it by about -0.17.
At h0, the combined signed point contribution from foreign bank-sector Treasury legs is about -0.41 in the direct component read.
